name: Emma
concern_text: Parents want a home plan for communication, motor skills, and daily routines.

asked.question: talks with you in conversation using at least two back and forth exchangess
ask who or what or where or why questions like where is mommy or where is daddy
says about 50 words
say two or more words toghether with one action word like doggie run
strings items together like large beads or macaroni
puts on some clothes by himself like loose pants or a jacket
uses hands to twist things like turning doorknobs or unscrewing lids
takes some clothes off by himself like loose pants or an open jacket
calms down within 10 minutes after you leave her like at childcare drop off
notices other children and joins them to play
plays next to other children and sometimes plays with them
shows you what she can do by saying look at me
draws a circle when you show her how
avoids touching hot objects like the stove when you warn her
uses things to pretend like feeding a block to a doll as if it were food
shows simple problem solving skills like standing on a small stool to reach something

speech_language.title: Picture Exchange Conversation
speech_language.why_this_fits: Encourages back-and-forth communication using visual supports.
speech_language.instructions: Use picture cards to represent common objects or actions. Take turns asking questions using the cards, such as 'What do you want?' or 'Where is the dog?'.
speech_language.materials: Picture cards (can be homemade or printed), a small box to hold the cards.
speech_language.adaptation: For limited speech, use a communication device or app to select pictures.

speech_language.title: Word and Action Game
speech_language.instructions: Say a word and perform an action (e.g., 'jump' while jumping). Encourage Emma to repeat the word and action.
speech_language.materials: None required, just space to move.
speech_language.adaptation: Use gestures or visual prompts if Emma struggles to say the words.

speech_language.title: Story Time with Questions
speech_language.instructions: Read a short story and pause to ask Emma questions like 'Who is this?' or 'What happens next?'.
speech_language.adaptation: Use simple yes/no questions or point to pictures for her to respond.

speech_language.title: Sing and Sign
speech_language.why_this_fits: Supports language development through music and gestures.
speech_language.instructions: Choose a simple song and incorporate hand signs for key words. Sing together.
speech_language.adaptation: Use visual aids or props to represent the words in the song.

speech_language.title: Labeling Household Items
speech_language.why_this_fits: Encourages vocabulary building in a familiar context.
speech_language.instructions: Walk around the house and label items together (e.g., 'table', 'chair').
speech_language.adaptation: Use picture labels if Emma has difficulty with words.

motor.title: Bead Stringing
motor.why_this_fits: Enhances fine motor skills through a fun activity.
motor.instructions: Provide large beads and a string. Encourage Emma to string the beads in a pattern.
motor.adaptation: Use a thicker string or pipe cleaner for easier handling.

motor.title: Dress-Up Challenge
motor.why_this_fits: Promotes independence in dressing skills.
motor.instructions: Lay out loose clothing items and encourage Emma to put them on herself.
motor.adaptation: Assist with tricky parts like buttons or zippers.

motor.title: Twist and Turn
motor.why_this_fits: Develops hand strength and coordination.
motor.instructions: Provide various containers with lids. Encourage Emma to twist and turn the lids on and off.
motor.materials: Containers with different lids (e.g., jars, bottles).
motor.adaptation: Use larger containers for easier manipulation.

motor.title: Obstacle Course
motor.why_this_fits: Encourages gross motor skills through movement.
motor.instructions: Set up a simple obstacle course with pillows, chairs, and toys to crawl over or around.
motor.adaptation: Simplify the course by reducing the number of obstacles.

motor.title: Ball Toss
motor.why_this_fits: Improves hand-eye coordination and gross motor skills.
motor.instructions: Use a soft ball to toss back and forth. Encourage Emma to catch and throw.
motor.adaptation: Use a larger ball for easier catching.

social_communication.title: Play Date Role Play
social_communication.instructions: Role-play different scenarios with dolls or toys, such as sharing or asking to play.
social_communication.adaptation: Use simple prompts to guide Emma in the role play.

social_communication.title: Emotion Charades
social_communication.instructions: Act out different emotions and have Emma guess what they are.
social_communication.adaptation: Use picture cards of emotions for visual support.

social_communication.title: Group Play with Siblings
social_communication.instructions: Engage in a simple game with siblings that requires taking turns.
social_communication.materials: Board game or simple card game.
social_communication.adaptation: Simplify rules to ensure Emma can participate.

social_communication.title: Show and Tell
social_communication.instructions: Encourage Emma to bring a favorite toy and talk about it.
social_communication.adaptation: Provide sentence starters to help her express herself.

social_communication.title: Follow the Leader
social_communication.instructions: Take turns being the leader and doing actions for each other to follow.
social_communication.adaptation: Use simple actions that Emma can easily imitate.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Simple Puzzles
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Provide a simple puzzle and encourage Emma to fit the pieces together.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use larger pieces for easier handling.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Pretend Play with Food
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Enhances imaginative play and cognitive skills.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Use toy food and encourage Emma to 'feed' a doll or stuffed animal.
adaptive_cognitive.materials: Toy food and a doll or stuffed animal.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use real food items for more engagement if safe.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Sorting Objects
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Provide various objects and ask Emma to sort them by color or size.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use fewer items to maintain focus.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Drawing with Guidance
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Encourages creativity and fine motor skills.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Show Emma how to draw simple shapes and encourage her to try.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use thicker crayons for easier grip.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Treasure Hunt
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Hide objects around the room and give Emma clues to find them.
adaptive_cognitive.materials: Small toys or objects to hide.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use visual clues or pictures to guide her.

profile_text_for_prompt:
Speech / Language estimated around 24-30 months. Evidence: 36m: with_help to 'talks with you in conversation using at least two back and forth exchangess'; 36m: with_help to 'ask who or what or where or why questions like where is mommy or where is daddy'; 30m: sometimes to 'says about 50 words'; 30m: sometimes to 'say two or more words toghether with one action word like doggie run'
Motor estimated around 30-36 months. Evidence: 36m: sometimes to 'strings items together like large beads or macaroni'; 36m: sometimes to 'puts on some clothes by himself like loose pants or a jacket'; 30m: yes to 'uses hands to twist things like turning doorknobs or unscrewing lids'; 30m: yes to 'takes some clothes off by himself like loose pants or an open jacket'
Social / Communication estimated around 30-36 months. Evidence: 36m: sometimes to 'calms down within 10 minutes after you leave her like at childcare drop off'; 36m: sometimes to 'notices other children and joins them to play'; 30m: yes to 'plays next to other children and sometimes plays with them'; 30m: yes to 'shows you what she can do by saying look at me'
Adaptive / Cognitive estimated around 24-30 months. Evidence: 36m: with_help to 'draws a circle when you show her how'; 36m: with_help to 'avoids touching hot objects like the stove when you warn her'; 30m: sometimes to 'uses things to pretend like feeding a block to a doll as if it were food'; 30m: sometimes to 'shows simple problem solving skills like standing on a small stool to reach something'
note: CDC table only goes to 60 months, so children older than 60 months start from the 60-month band as an anchor.

name: Noah
concern_text: Parents report limited back-and-forth play, speech delay, and rigid routines.

asked.question: tells a storys she heard or made up with at least two events for example cat was stuck in a tree and firefighter saved it
answers simple questions about a book or a story after you read or tell it to her
says sentences with four or more words
says some words from a song or story or nursery rhyme
talks with you in conversation using at least two back and forth exchangess
ask who or what or where or why questions like where is mommy or where is daddy
buttons some buttons
hops on one foot
follows rules or takesake turns when playing games with other  children
sings dances or acts for you
pretends to be something else during play like teacher or superhero or a dog
asks to go play with children if none are around like can I play with Alex
calms down within 10 minutes after you leave her like at childcare drop off
notices other children and joins them to play
plays next to other children and sometimes plays with them
shows you what she can do by saying look at me
counts to 10
names some numbers between 1 and 5 when you point to them
names a few colors or items
tell what comes next in a well known story
draws a circle when you show her how
avoids touching hot objects like the stove when you warn her

speech_language.title: Story Sequencing with Pictures
speech_language.instructions: Use a set of picture cards that tell a simple story. Ask Noah to arrange them in order and describe what happens in each picture.
speech_language.materials: Picture cards or printed images from a story.
speech_language.adaptation: If Noah struggles with verbalizing, encourage him to point to the pictures and use single words or phrases.

speech_language.title: Song Repetition
speech_language.instructions: Play a simple song and sing along. Pause after each line and encourage Noah to repeat the last word or phrase.
speech_language.adaptation: Use visual aids or gestures to help him understand the song's content.

speech_language.title: Question and Answer Game
speech_language.why_this_fits: Promotes answering questions and conversation skills.
speech_language.instructions: Ask Noah simple questions about his day or favorite toys, encouraging him to respond with short sentences.
speech_language.adaptation: Use visual prompts or choices if he has difficulty answering.

speech_language.title: Labeling Objects
speech_language.instructions: Choose common objects around the house and ask Noah to name them. You can model the words first.
speech_language.adaptation: Use gestures or point to the objects to support his responses.

speech_language.title: Story Time with Questions
speech_language.instructions: Read a short story and pause to ask Noah questions about it, encouraging him to answer.
speech_language.adaptation: Use yes/no questions or multiple-choice options to make it easier for him to respond.

motor.title: Obstacle Course
motor.why_this_fits: Improves gross motor skills and coordination.
motor.instructions: Set up a simple obstacle course using cushions, chairs, and toys for Noah to navigate.
motor.adaptation: Modify the course to be easier if he has difficulty with certain movements.

motor.title: Buttoning Practice
motor.why_this_fits: Enhances fine motor skills and independence.
motor.instructions: Provide a shirt or fabric with buttons for Noah to practice buttoning and unbuttoning.
motor.adaptation: Use larger buttons or a button board to make it easier.

motor.title: Ball Toss
motor.why_this_fits: Develops hand-eye coordination.
motor.instructions: Play a simple game of tossing a soft ball back and forth with Noah.
motor.adaptation: Reduce the distance between you if he has difficulty catching.

motor.title: Drawing with Crayons
motor.why_this_fits: Encourages fine motor control and creativity.
motor.instructions: Provide crayons and paper for Noah to draw freely or follow simple prompts like drawing shapes.
motor.adaptation: Use thicker crayons or markers if he has difficulty gripping.

motor.title: Simon Says
motor.why_this_fits: Enhances listening skills and motor planning.
motor.instructions: Play a game of Simon Says with various movements (e.g., jump, clap, spin).
motor.adaptation: Use visual cues or demonstrate the actions for clarity.

social_communication.title: Turn-Taking with Toys
social_communication.instructions: Play with a toy that requires turns (like stacking blocks) and model taking turns with Noah.
social_communication.adaptation: Use a timer to signal turns if he struggles with waiting.

social_communication.title: Role Play with Puppets
social_communication.why_this_fits: Fosters imaginative play and social interaction.
social_communication.instructions: Use puppets to act out simple scenarios, encouraging Noah to join in the dialogue.
social_communication.adaptation: Model the dialogue first and encourage him to mimic or respond.

social_communication.title: Group Play with Family
social_communication.why_this_fits: Promotes social interaction in a familiar setting.
social_communication.instructions: Engage in a simple board game or card game with family members, encouraging Noah to participate.
social_communication.adaptation: Simplify the rules or use visual aids to help him understand.

social_communication.title: Emotion Charades
social_communication.instructions: Take turns acting out different emotions while the other guesses what they are.
social_communication.adaptation: Use visual cues or model the emotions for him to imitate.

social_communication.title: Simple Group Activities
social_communication.instructions: Organize a simple group activity like a craft or a game with a small group of familiar children.
social_communication.adaptation: Provide clear instructions and support to help him engage.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Counting Objects
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Supports number recognition and counting skills.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Use small toys or snacks to practice counting together.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use visual aids or number cards to assist him in counting.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Color Sorting
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Enhances color recognition and categorization skills.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Provide colored objects and ask Noah to sort them into groups by color.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use fewer colors to simplify the task if needed.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Story Prediction
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Read a familiar story and pause to ask Noah what he thinks will happen next.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Offer choices or visual prompts to help him formulate predictions.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Matching Games
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Play a simple matching game with cards or objects.
adaptive_cognitive.materials: Matching cards or pairs of objects.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use fewer pairs to make the game easier and more engaging.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Daily Routine Chart
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Supports understanding of daily activities and time management.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Create a visual chart of Noah's daily activities and review it together.
adaptive_cognitive.materials: Paper, markers, and pictures of daily activities.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use simple images and limit the number of activities to avoid overwhelming him.

profile_text_for_prompt:
Speech / Language estimated around 36-48 months. Evidence: 60m: no to 'tells a storys she heard or made up with at least two events for example cat was stuck in a tree and firefighter saved it'; 60m: no to 'answers simple questions about a book or a story after you read or tell it to her'; 48m: with_help to 'says sentences with four or more words'; 48m: with_help to 'says some words from a song or story or nursery rhyme'
Motor estimated around 60-60 months. Evidence: 60m: yes to 'buttons some buttons'; 60m: yes to 'hops on one foot'
Social / Communication estimated around 30-36 months. Evidence: 60m: no to 'follows rules or takesake turns when playing games with other  children'; 60m: no to 'sings dances or acts for you'; 48m: no to 'pretends to be something else during play like teacher or superhero or a dog'; 48m: no to 'asks to go play with children if none are around like can I play with Alex'
Adaptive / Cognitive estimated around 36-48 months. Evidence: 60m: no to 'counts to 10'; 60m: no to 'names some numbers between 1 and 5 when you point to them'; 48m: sometimes to 'names a few colors or items'; 48m: sometimes to 'tell what comes next in a well known story'
note: CDC table only goes to 60 months, so children older than 60 months start from the 60-month band as an anchor.

name: Liam
concern_text: Parents want better daily routines, attention, task completion, and social flexibility.

asked.question: says sentences with four or more words
says some words from a song or story or nursery rhyme
talks with you in conversation using at least two back and forth exchangess
ask who or what or where or why questions like where is mommy or where is daddy
catches a large ball most of the times
serves herself food or pours water with adult supervision
strings items together like large beads or macaroni
puts on some clothes by himself like loose pants or a jacket
pretends to be something else during play like teacher or superhero or a dog
asks to go play with children if none are around like can I play with Alex
calms down within 10 minutes after you leave her like at childcare drop off
notices other children and joins them to play
plays next to other children and sometimes plays with them
shows you what she can do by saying look at me
names a few colors or items
tell what comes next in a well known story
draws a circle when you show her how
avoids touching hot objects like the stove when you warn her

speech_language.title: Story Time with Questions
speech_language.instructions: Read a short story and pause to ask Liam questions about it, encouraging him to answer in complete sentences.
speech_language.adaptation: If Liam struggles to answer, provide sentence starters or options to choose from.

speech_language.title: Sing Along
speech_language.why_this_fits: Promotes language through music and repetition.
speech_language.instructions: Choose a familiar song and sing together, encouraging him to fill in the missing words.
speech_language.adaptation: Use visuals or gestures to help him remember the words.

speech_language.title: Picture Description
speech_language.instructions: Show Liam a picture and ask him to describe what he sees in four or more words.
speech_language.materials: A colorful picture or a photo.
speech_language.adaptation: Provide prompts or ask yes/no questions to guide his responses.

speech_language.title: Role Play Conversations
speech_language.instructions: Act out a simple scenario (like ordering food) and take turns speaking.
speech_language.materials: Props for the role play (like toy food).
speech_language.adaptation: Use a script or cue cards to help him remember what to say.

speech_language.title: Word Hunt
speech_language.why_this_fits: Encourages vocabulary building and word recognition.
speech_language.instructions: Go on a scavenger hunt around the house to find items that start with specific letters.
speech_language.materials: List of letters, paper, and a bag for collecting items.
speech_language.adaptation: Limit the number of items to find to keep it manageable.

motor.title: Ball Toss
motor.why_this_fits: Improves hand-eye coordination and gross motor skills.
motor.instructions: Stand a few feet apart and toss a soft ball back and forth.
motor.adaptation: Start with a larger ball and reduce the distance as he improves.

motor.title: Bead Stringing
motor.why_this_fits: Enhances fine motor skills and concentration.
motor.instructions: String large beads onto a shoelace or string.
motor.adaptation: Use a thicker string for easier handling.

motor.title: Dress Up Challenge
motor.why_this_fits: Promotes independence in dressing skills.
motor.instructions: Encourage Liam to put on a jacket or loose pants by himself.
motor.adaptation: Provide verbal cues or assistance as needed.

motor.title: Obstacle Course
motor.why_this_fits: Develops gross motor skills through movement.
motor.instructions: Set up a simple obstacle course using cushions and chairs to crawl under and climb over.
motor.materials: Cushions, chairs, and space to move.
motor.adaptation: Adjust the course to be easier or more challenging based on his ability.

motor.title: Pouring Practice
motor.why_this_fits: Enhances fine motor skills and independence.
motor.instructions: Practice pouring water from one container to another with supervision.
motor.adaptation: Use a larger container to make it easier to pour.

social_communication.title: Playdate Planning
social_communication.instructions: Help Liam invite a friend over and plan an activity together.
social_communication.adaptation: Role-play the invitation process to build confidence.

social_communication.title: Emotion Charades
social_communication.why_this_fits: Develops empathy and understanding of emotions.
social_communication.instructions: Take turns acting out different emotions while the other guesses.
social_communication.materials: Emotion cards or a list of emotions.
social_communication.adaptation: Use visuals to help him understand the emotions better.

social_communication.title: Group Story Creation
social_communication.instructions: Take turns adding a sentence to create a story together.
social_communication.materials: A notebook to write the story down.
social_communication.adaptation: Provide prompts or sentence starters to help him contribute.

social_communication.title: Compliment Circle
social_communication.instructions: Sit in a circle and take turns giving each other compliments.
social_communication.adaptation: Model compliments first to guide him.

social_communication.title: Turn-Taking Games
social_communication.instructions: Play a simple board game that requires taking turns.
social_communication.adaptation: Use a timer to help him understand when it's his turn.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Color Sorting
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Sort objects by color into different containers.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Provide fewer colors to sort if he gets overwhelmed.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Story Sequencing
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Use picture cards from a familiar story and arrange them in order.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Provide a model sequence to follow.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Matching Game
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Play a simple matching game with cards or objects.
adaptive_cognitive.materials: Matching cards or pairs of objects.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Start with fewer pairs to keep it manageable.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Simple Puzzles
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Complete a simple jigsaw puzzle together.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Choose puzzles with fewer pieces to start.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Cooking Together
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Encourages following directions and cognitive skills.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Follow a simple recipe together, measuring and mixing ingredients.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use visual steps or a video to guide the process.

profile_text_for_prompt:
Speech / Language estimated around 36-48 months. Evidence: 48m: with_help to 'says sentences with four or more words'; 48m: with_help to 'says some words from a song or story or nursery rhyme'; 36m: yes to 'talks with you in conversation using at least two back and forth exchangess'; 36m: yes to 'ask who or what or where or why questions like where is mommy or where is daddy'
Motor estimated around 36-48 months. Evidence: 48m: with_help to 'catches a large ball most of the times'; 48m: with_help to 'serves herself food or pours water with adult supervision'; 36m: yes to 'strings items together like large beads or macaroni'; 36m: yes to 'puts on some clothes by himself like loose pants or a jacket'
Social / Communication estimated around 24-30 months. Evidence: 48m: no to 'pretends to be something else during play like teacher or superhero or a dog'; 48m: no to 'asks to go play with children if none are around like can I play with Alex'; 36m: with_help to 'calms down within 10 minutes after you leave her like at childcare drop off'; 36m: with_help to 'notices other children and joins them to play'
Adaptive / Cognitive estimated around 36-48 months. Evidence: 48m: with_help to 'names a few colors or items'; 48m: with_help to 'tell what comes next in a well known story'; 36m: yes to 'draws a circle when you show her how'; 36m: yes to 'avoids touching hot objects like the stove when you warn her'
note: CDC table only goes to 60 months, so children older than 60 months start from the 60-month band as an anchor.

name: Maya
concern_text: Parents are most concerned about communication frustration and limited expressive language.

asked.question: says sentences with four or more words
says some words from a song or story or nursery rhyme
talks with you in conversation using at least two back and forth exchangess
ask who or what or where or why questions like where is mommy or where is daddy
says about 50 words
say two or more words toghether with one action word like doggie run
catches a large ball most of the times
serves herself food or pours water with adult supervision
buttons some buttons
hops on one foot
pretends to be something else during play like teacher or superhero or a dog
asks to go play with children if none are around like can I play with Alex
calms down within 10 minutes after you leave her like at childcare drop off
notices other children and joins them to play
names a few colors or items
tell what comes next in a well known story
counts to 10
names some numbers between 1 and 5 when you point to them

speech_language.title: Storytime with Props
speech_language.instructions: Choose a favorite story and use props to act it out. Encourage Maya to describe what is happening.
speech_language.materials: Storybook, simple props (toys or household items related to the story).
speech_language.adaptation: If speech is limited, prompt her with questions about the story using visual cues.

speech_language.title: Sing and Sign
speech_language.why_this_fits: Combines music with visual support to enhance communication.
speech_language.instructions: Choose a simple song and incorporate basic signs for key words. Sing together and encourage her to use signs.
speech_language.materials: Lyrics of a simple song, visual aids for signs.
speech_language.adaptation: Use gestures or pictures to support understanding and expression.

speech_language.title: Picture Exchange Communication
speech_language.why_this_fits: Helps express needs and wants visually.
speech_language.instructions: Create a set of pictures representing common items or activities. Encourage her to use them to communicate.
speech_language.materials: Printed pictures, a small box or board to hold them.
speech_language.adaptation: Use fewer pictures to avoid overwhelming her and focus on her favorites.

speech_language.title: Descriptive Play
speech_language.instructions: While playing with toys, encourage Maya to describe what she is doing or what the toys are.
speech_language.adaptation: Model sentences for her to repeat or fill in the blanks.

speech_language.title: Emotion Charades
speech_language.why_this_fits: Promotes understanding and expression of emotions.
speech_language.instructions: Act out different emotions and have her guess them. Discuss times when she feels these emotions.
speech_language.adaptation: Use visual aids to help her identify and express emotions.

motor.title: Obstacle Course
motor.why_this_fits: Enhances gross motor skills through fun physical activity.
motor.instructions: Set up a simple obstacle course using cushions, chairs, and toys. Encourage her to navigate through it.
motor.adaptation: Simplify the course by reducing obstacles if she struggles.

motor.title: Ball Toss
motor.why_this_fits: Improves hand-eye coordination and motor skills.
motor.instructions: Play catch using a soft ball. Start close and gradually increase the distance.
motor.adaptation: Use a larger ball for easier catching and throwing.

motor.title: Water Pouring
motor.why_this_fits: Develops fine motor skills and hand strength.
motor.instructions: Provide a small pitcher and cups. Encourage her to pour water from one cup to another.
motor.adaptation: Use a larger pitcher for easier handling.

motor.title: Building Blocks
motor.why_this_fits: Enhances fine motor skills and creativity.
motor.instructions: Encourage her to build structures using blocks. Discuss what she is building.
motor.adaptation: Use larger blocks for easier manipulation.

motor.title: Dance Party
motor.why_this_fits: Promotes gross motor skills through movement and rhythm.
motor.instructions: Play her favorite music and encourage her to dance freely. Join in to model movements.
motor.adaptation: Keep movements simple and follow her lead.

social_communication.title: Role Play
social_communication.why_this_fits: Encourages imaginative play and social interaction.
social_communication.instructions: Pretend to be different characters (like a teacher or doctor) and have her join in the role play.
social_communication.adaptation: Use simple roles and provide prompts to guide her participation.

social_communication.title: Playdate Planning
social_communication.why_this_fits: Encourages her to express desires for social interaction.
social_communication.instructions: Discuss potential playdates with friends. Help her express who she wants to invite.
social_communication.adaptation: Use pictures of friends to help her communicate her choices.

social_communication.title: Group Games
social_communication.instructions: Play simple group games like 'Simon Says' or 'Duck, Duck, Goose' with family.
social_communication.adaptation: Simplify rules and provide physical assistance if needed.

social_communication.title: Emotion Sharing
social_communication.why_this_fits: Encourages discussion about feelings and empathy.
social_communication.instructions: Share a story or experience and discuss how it made her feel. Encourage her to share her own feelings.
social_communication.adaptation: Use visual aids to help her express emotions.

social_communication.title: Friendship Art
social_communication.instructions: Create a drawing or craft for a friend. Discuss what they like about that friend.
social_communication.adaptation: Provide examples to guide her in expressing her thoughts.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Color Hunt
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Enhances color recognition and cognitive skills.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Go on a color hunt around the house. Ask her to find items of a specific color.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Limit to one color at a time to simplify the task.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Story Sequencing
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Use pictures from a familiar story and ask her to arrange them in order.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Provide hints or model the sequence if she struggles.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Counting Objects
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Promotes counting skills and number recognition.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Count everyday objects together, like toys or snacks. Encourage her to count along.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use fewer objects to avoid overwhelming her.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Matching Game
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Create a simple matching game with pairs of pictures or cards. Take turns matching them.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use fewer pairs to keep the game manageable.

adaptive_cognitive.title: What Comes Next?
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Tell a familiar story and pause before the end. Ask her what she thinks will happen next.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Provide choices to help her make predictions.

profile_text_for_prompt:
Speech / Language estimated around 30-36 months. Evidence: 48m: no to 'says sentences with four or more words'; 48m: no to 'says some words from a song or story or nursery rhyme'; 36m: sometimes to 'talks with you in conversation using at least two back and forth exchangess'; 36m: sometimes to 'ask who or what or where or why questions like where is mommy or where is daddy'
Motor estimated around 48-60 months. Evidence: 48m: yes to 'catches a large ball most of the times'; 48m: yes to 'serves herself food or pours water with adult supervision'; 60m: with_help to 'buttons some buttons'; 60m: with_help to 'hops on one foot'
Social / Communication estimated around 36-48 months. Evidence: 48m: sometimes to 'pretends to be something else during play like teacher or superhero or a dog'; 48m: sometimes to 'asks to go play with children if none are around like can I play with Alex'; 36m: yes to 'calms down within 10 minutes after you leave her like at childcare drop off'; 36m: yes to 'notices other children and joins them to play'
Adaptive / Cognitive estimated around 48-60 months. Evidence: 48m: yes to 'names a few colors or items'; 48m: yes to 'tell what comes next in a well known story'; 60m: with_help to 'counts to 10'; 60m: with_help to 'names some numbers between 1 and 5 when you point to them'
note: CDC table only goes to 60 months, so children older than 60 months start from the 60-month band as an anchor.

name: Sofia
concern_text: Parents want a realistic home plan for movement, communication, and independence.

asked.question: points to things in a book when you ask like where is the bear
says at least two words together like more milk
tries to say three or more words besides mama or dada
follows one step directions without any gestures like giving you the toy when you say give it to me
kicks a ball
runs
uses hands to twist things like turning doorknobs or unscrewing lids
takes some clothes off by himself like loose pants or an open jacket
strings items together like large beads or macaroni
puts on some clothes by himself like loose pants or a jacket
notices when others are hurt or upset like pausing or looking sad when someone is crying
looks at your face to see how to react in a new situation
moves away from you but looks to make sure you are close by
points to show you something interesting
holds something in one hand while using the other hand for example holding a container and taking the lid off
tries to use switches or knobs or buttons on a toy
copies you doing chores like sweeping with a broom
plays with toys in a simple way like pushing a toy car
tries to use things the right way like a phone or a cup or a book
stacks at least two small objects like blocks

speech_language.title: Book Pointing
speech_language.why_this_fits: Encourages vocabulary and comprehension through interactive reading.
speech_language.instructions: Choose a picture book and ask Sofia to point to specific animals or objects when you say their names.
speech_language.materials: A picture book with clear images.
speech_language.adaptation: If verbal communication is challenging, use gestures or provide choices to help her point.

speech_language.title: Two-Word Phrases
speech_language.why_this_fits: Supports language development by encouraging word combinations.
speech_language.instructions: Model two-word phrases like 'more juice' or 'big truck' and encourage Sofia to repeat them.
speech_language.adaptation: Use visual aids or objects to prompt her to say the phrases.

speech_language.title: Follow the Direction
speech_language.instructions: Give Sofia simple one-step directions like 'give me the ball' and encourage her to follow them.
speech_language.materials: A ball or any small toy.
speech_language.adaptation: Use physical prompts or gestures to guide her if she struggles.

speech_language.title: Sound Imitation
speech_language.instructions: Make animal sounds and encourage Sofia to imitate them.
speech_language.adaptation: Use visual cues like pictures of animals to support her understanding.

speech_language.title: Action Words
speech_language.instructions: Use toys to demonstrate actions (like 'jump' with a toy figure) and encourage Sofia to say the action word.
speech_language.adaptation: Use hand-over-hand assistance to help her produce the words.

motor.title: Ball Kicking
motor.why_this_fits: Develops gross motor skills through play.
motor.instructions: Set up a soft ball and encourage Sofia to kick it back and forth with you.
motor.adaptation: If kicking is challenging, assist her by holding her leg or using a rolling motion.

motor.title: Obstacle Course
motor.why_this_fits: Encourages movement and coordination.
motor.instructions: Create a simple obstacle course with cushions and soft toys for Sofia to navigate.
motor.adaptation: Modify the course to include only low obstacles that she can easily maneuver.

motor.title: Twisting Lids
motor.why_this_fits: Enhances fine motor skills through manipulation.
motor.instructions: Provide jars with lids and encourage Sofia to practice twisting them open and closed.
motor.adaptation: Use larger jars or assist her hands to help with the twisting motion.

motor.title: Dress-Up Time
motor.why_this_fits: Promotes independence in dressing skills.
motor.instructions: Encourage Sofia to practice putting on and taking off loose clothing items.
motor.adaptation: Use clothing with larger openings or provide assistance as needed.

motor.title: Balloon Toss
motor.why_this_fits: Improves hand-eye coordination and reaction time.
motor.instructions: Gently toss a balloon back and forth with Sofia, encouraging her to catch it.
motor.adaptation: If catching is difficult, allow her to hit the balloon instead.

social_communication.title: Emotion Faces
social_communication.why_this_fits: Helps Sofia recognize and respond to emotions.
social_communication.instructions: Show her pictures of faces expressing different emotions and discuss them.
social_communication.materials: Pictures of faces or emotion cards.
social_communication.adaptation: Use fewer pictures or focus on one emotion at a time to maintain attention.

social_communication.title: Turn-Taking Game
social_communication.why_this_fits: Encourages sharing and waiting for her turn.
social_communication.instructions: Play a simple game like rolling a ball back and forth, taking turns.
social_communication.adaptation: Use a timer to signal turns if she has difficulty waiting.

social_communication.title: Look and React
social_communication.instructions: Make different facial expressions and encourage Sofia to mimic or react to them.
social_communication.adaptation: Use a mirror to help her see her own expressions.

social_communication.title: Pointing Out Interests
social_communication.why_this_fits: Encourages her to share what she finds interesting.
social_communication.instructions: Walk around the house and ask Sofia to point to things she likes.
social_communication.adaptation: Use hand-over-hand guidance to help her point if needed.

social_communication.title: Story Time Reactions
social_communication.why_this_fits: Encourages engagement and emotional responses to stories.
social_communication.instructions: Read a short story and pause to ask Sofia how she thinks characters feel.
social_communication.adaptation: Simplify the story or use props to illustrate emotions.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Simple Chores
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Involve Sofia in simple chores like putting away toys.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use a visual schedule to guide her through the steps.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Toy Sorting
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Provide different types of toys and ask Sofia to sort them into groups.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Limit the number of toys to avoid overwhelming her.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Container Play
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Enhances problem-solving and fine motor skills.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Give Sofia containers and various items to practice putting in and taking out.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use larger items that are easier for her to grasp.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Follow the Leader
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Encourages imitation and understanding of sequences.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Model simple actions for Sofia to imitate, like clapping or stomping.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use hand-over-hand assistance to help her with the actions.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Color Matching
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Promotes recognition of colors and matching skills.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Provide colored objects and ask Sofia to match them to colored cards.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use fewer colors to keep her focused and engaged.

profile_text_for_prompt:
Speech / Language estimated around 15-18 months. Evidence: 24m: with_help to 'points to things in a book when you ask like where is the bear'; 24m: with_help to 'says at least two words together like more milk'; 18m: sometimes to 'tries to say three or more words besides mama or dada'; 18m: sometimes to 'follows one step directions without any gestures like giving you the toy when you say give it to me'
Motor estimated around 30-36 months. Evidence: 24m: yes to 'kicks a ball'; 24m: yes to 'runs'; 30m: yes to 'uses hands to twist things like turning doorknobs or unscrewing lids'; 30m: yes to 'takes some clothes off by himself like loose pants or an open jacket'
Social / Communication estimated around 15-18 months. Evidence: 24m: with_help to 'notices when others are hurt or upset like pausing or looking sad when someone is crying'; 24m: with_help to 'looks at your face to see how to react in a new situation'; 18m: sometimes to 'moves away from you but looks to make sure you are close by'; 18m: sometimes to 'points to show you something interesting'
Adaptive / Cognitive estimated around 12-15 months. Evidence: 24m: no to 'holds something in one hand while using the other hand for example holding a container and taking the lid off'; 24m: no to 'tries to use switches or knobs or buttons on a toy'; 18m: with_help to 'copies you doing chores like sweeping with a broom'; 18m: with_help to 'plays with toys in a simple way like pushing a toy car'
note: CDC table only goes to 60 months, so children older than 60 months start from the 60-month band as an anchor.
